preview.summary: Colombia, a top-20 FIFA side packed with European talent, meet a DR Congo team making their first World Cup appearance in 52 years. Nestor Lorenzo's Colombia will be favored, but the Leopards bring pace and directness through a dangerous frontline.
preview.homeForm: Colombia arrive ranked inside the FIFA top 20 and confident of progressing from Group K alongside Portugal.
preview.awayForm: DR Congo, ranked around 46th, return to the World Cup for the first time in 52 years.
preview.homeKey: Luis Diaz, Bayern Munich winger and Colombia's biggest attacking threat.
preview.awayKey: Yoane Wissa, Newcastle forward bringing pace and clinical finishing.
storylines: DR Congo's first World Cup in over half a century
Luis Diaz's Bayern form versus a debutant-feel Congo side
James Rodriguez orchestrates Colombia's creative play
preview.h2h: The two nations have no significant competitive history at World Cup level.
